page: Microsoft Azure D8alds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine from the Daldv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache per core. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is provided via a 472 GB local disk, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. No GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing placing the instance in the top 10% with a score of 3080.69, and Geekbench single-core scoring in the top 25% at 2105.0. Multi-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling within the average range. Memory bandwidth is also average, measuring 39.09 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 27.76 GB/sec for large block write operations. Text processing performance is a notable highlight, ranking in the top tier (top 10%) with a Geekbench text processing score of 2750.0. For lightweight language model tasks, the server achieves a strong text generation speed of 259.22 tokens/sec on a small 135M model, though performance drops to a weak 23.53 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a medium 7B model.

Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the D8alds_v6 is positioned as a compute-oriented instance with a lower memory footprint. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require high single-core CPU performance and local storage but do not demand massive memory allocations. The inclusion of a 472 GB local disk provides a cost-effective alternative to relying solely on network-attached storage for temporary files or scratch space. Tradeoffs include a relatively low memory capacity of 16.0 GB, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive databases or large-scale data analytics. The instance is best suited for single-threaded processing tasks, software compilation, lightweight web serving, and small-scale text processing applications.
description: Microsoft Azure D8alds_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine powered by an 8-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 16.0 GB of memory (2.0 GB per core) and a 472 GB local disk with Premium Storage capabilities. The instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core tests and Geekbench text processing. However, its multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities remain in the average tier. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server is a compute-oriented option that offers qualitative cost efficiency for CPU-bound workloads requiring local storage but limited memory. It is well-suited for software compilation, text processing, lightweight web serving, and single-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8alds_v6 is an 8-vCPU virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC processor, 16 GB of RAM, and a 472 GB local disk, delivering strong single-core CPU and text processing performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8alds_v6 is an 8-vCPU AMD EPYC virtual machine with 16 GB RAM and 472 GB local storage, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A compute-focused virtual machine featuring high single-core performance, dedicated AMD EPYC processors, and integrated local storage for CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM and a 472 GB local disk
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Compute-oriented 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio for memory-lean workloads
Best suited for software compilation, text processing, and lightweight web serving
